title: Vilfa procera
intro: Vilfa procera, also known as mountain rice grass, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Vilfa procera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Vilfa procera include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and support immune function.
taxonomy: Vilfa procera is classified as a species of grass within the genus Vilfa. It belongs to the family Poaceae. The genus Vilfa is part of the subfamily Pooideae.
